author: Maxim-Doronin
title: Windows CI fails with ECONNREFUSED downloading artifacts and Electron cleanup Permission Denied
signature: artifact download failed unable to make request ECONNREFUSED|Network
failed_jobs: OpenVINO tokenizers extension / OpenVINO tokenizers extension
key_errors: Failed to ListArtifacts: Unable to make request: ECONNREFUSED
root_cause: Two independent infrastructure failures: (1) The tokenizers job runner could not reach GitHub's artifact API (ECONNREFUSED), preventing the openvino_wheels artifact download, which in turn caused the .whl upload to fail. (2) The JS API E2E test cleanup hook failed with Permission Denied trying to delete electron.exe on Windows — the actual 2 E2E tests passed. Both are transient infrastructure issues unrelated to code changes in PR #36796.
recommended_actions: Re-run failed jobs — both failures are transient
Monitor ECONNREFUSED rate on Windows self-hosted runners
Fix JS E2E cleanup to use Windows-safe cleanup (e.g., rd /s /q) instead of rm -rf
